summary:
The sublingual space is primarily bounded inferiorly by the body of the mandible as well as being separated laterally by the mylohyoid muscle along with associated fascia; thus, it does not exclusively rely upon only one structure for demarcation but involves multiple anatomic elements including bone and soft tissue boundaries. Agent1 concludes based on retrieved information about both bony landmarks such as the body of the mandible and muscular/fascial divisions involving the mylohyoid layer separating these spaces effectively.
